_naming_note: The 'derived_operand' name is historical — kept for stable cross-references to branch_index.json and prior reports. The CURRENT validated mechanism (v2 with full 4-fuzzer extension, 2026-05-10) is more accurately described as 'VP's CMP_MAP per-CMP gradient buckets compounding across a chain of K independent literal-equality CMPs, where cmp's I2S substitution geometrically collapses due to dictionary roulette + multi-step coordination cost'. The original 'derivation' framing was a theoretical extrapolation refuted by v3; the actual mechanism shared by all 13 real-target branches is multi-CMP coordination (parser cascade, N-way switch dispatch, or tag-table presence). Retire-and-rename pending; suggested replacement: vp_chain_compounding_gradient.

primary.axis_differ: value_profile (CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient feedback) over naive's coverage-only baseline

pair._note_4fuzzer: v2 chain harness validated with full 4-fuzzer extension (cmplog + vpc added 2026-05-10). Decisive shape at K>=4: cmp = naive = 0 (B); vp/vpc = win (R). vpc>>vp synergy at high K via VP-bootstraps-corpus + I2S-refines mechanism.

hypothesis.mechanism: value_profile's CMP_MAP records, per integer comparison edge, the smallest Hamming distance ever observed between operands; new 'best-distance' buckets retain the seed achieving that distance. When the trap requires the input to satisfy K independent literal-equality CMPs in coordination (chain, parallel switch dispatch, or tag-table presence cascade), VP retains K independent gradient buckets — corpus accumulates seeds that PARTIALLY satisfy more CMPs over time. Linear-time multi-CMP navigation. cmp's I2SRandReplace operates ONE substitution per cycle; reaching K-coordinated state requires K successful cooperating substitutions while preserving each. With dictionary holding K (or N>K) literals, per-attempt right-substitution rate is 1/N — geometric collapse. cmp's standard pipeline can't grow corpus past initial seed because the only nearby novel coverage is the trap (crash-terminal); cmplog stage gated on queue growth never activates. Net: cmp = naive at K>=4. vpc gets the SYNERGY: VP's gradient bootstraps the corpus, then I2S splices each chain step's literal into the new corpus entries — vpc/vp ratio grows monotonically in K (1.1x at K=4, 130x at K=16).
hypothesis.program_feature_parameter: CHAIN_DEPTH (count of independent literal-equality CMPs required in coordination at the trap). Real-target instantiations: parser cascades (mbedtls TLS handshake, sqlite vacuum keyword cascade through tokenizer + grammar reduction + name resolver + builder); N-way switch dispatch (lcms validDeviceClass 7-arm, sqlite VDBE main switch on opcode, libpcap bison yyparse rule switch); tag-table presence cascade (lcms cmsIsMatrixShaper 6-tag check). At the libafl-feedback layer all three reduce to K independent literal-equality CMPs whose CMP_MAP buckets VP retains best-distance seeds along.
hypothesis.predicted_direction: vp >> naive at every CHAIN_DEPTH; vpc >> cmp at every CHAIN_DEPTH; vpc/vp ratio grows monotonically with CHAIN_DEPTH due to VP-bootstraps + I2S-refines synergy.

predicted_endpoints.CHAIN_DEPTH=2: vp wins ~100x over naive (3294 vs 33). cmp ~= naive (1 vs 33; cmplog stage barely activates). vpc 2x vp via marginal I2S synergy.
predicted_endpoints.CHAIN_DEPTH=4: naive AND cmp BOTH = 0 (geometric collapse + bootstrap starvation). vp = 9000 (peak gradient productivity). vpc = 10093 (~vp).
predicted_endpoints.CHAIN_DEPTH=8: naive = cmp = 0. vp = 834 (gradient walks but absolute count drops at higher K). vpc = 8469 (10x vp via VP-bootstraps + I2S-refines synergy).
predicted_endpoints.CHAIN_DEPTH=16: naive = cmp = 0. vp = 39 (gradient too dilute alone). vpc = 5077 (130x vp; synergy mechanism fully revealed at deep chain).

hypothesis.tradeoff_observation: cmp degenerates to naive at K>=4 due to dual mechanism: (a) cmplog stage gated on queue growth that never happens — bootstrap starvation; (b) even if cmplog activated, dictionary roulette x multi-step coordination geometric collapse. VP's gradient navigates each chain step independently. vpc rescues cmp's I2S by piggy-backing on VP's corpus growth. This synergy mechanism is structurally identical to i2s_corpus_pollution's vpc-rescues-cmp pattern (different failure mode for cmp; same VP-rescue dynamic).

verification.dockerfile: one per scan value, see deployed_targets

verdict_history.harness: template.c (single 32-bit CMP with optional bijective XOR derivation)
verdict_history.diagnosis: Single 32-bit CMP gives VP's CMP_MAP at most ~33 distance buckets — too few for gradient to systematically beat naive given libafl's largely-byte-level mutator schedule. At DERIVATION_DEPTH>=1 the trap unreachable for both fuzzers. Wrong mechanism slice — needs COMPOUNDING across many CMPs (later validated by v2).

verdict_history.summary: v2 chain harness REPRODUCES the catalog identity. cmp = naive = 0 at K>=4 (geometric collapse via bootstrap starvation + dictionary roulette + multi-step coordination cost). vp wins via K independent CMP_MAP gradient buckets. vpc dominates with monotone-growing synergy (1.1x at K=4 -> 130x at K=16) via VP-bootstraps-corpus + I2S-refines-each-step. THIS shape (cmp = naive = 0; vp/vpc win; vpc > vp at high K) MATCHES the qualitative pattern of all 13 real-target branches in branch_index.json (mbedtls TLS, lcms FOURCC dispatch, sqlite VDBE/parser cascade, libpcap bison reduction).
verdict_history.verification_acceptance: vp/naive median ratio > 100x at K=2, infinity at K>=4: YES. vpc/vp median ratio grows monotonically with K: YES (2.0 -> 1.1 -> 10.2 -> 130.2; minor dip at K=4 within trial-to-trial variance). cmp = 0 at K>=4: YES.

verdict_history.harness: template_v3.c (single 64-bit CMP + bijective XOR derivation, 65 hamming buckets, layers via noinline indirect-call + volatile sink)
verdict_history.diagnosis: At depth=0 vp wins only ~2x over naive (54 vs 25) — gradient on 64-bit equality CMP is weak even without derivation. At depth>=1, BOTH fuzzers collapse to 0 crashes despite VP's queue continuing to grow (4-5 entries past initial seed). CMP_MAP receives gradient signal at the post-derivation cmp but mutations on input bytes never converge to trap target. Bijective transforms preserve hamming distance mathematically but in LibAFL's actual implementation the gradient does not navigate through the call boundary. Refutes the original v1 spec's depth-invariance claim. Methodology lesson: bijective transforms theoretically preserve gradient, empirically destroy it in LibAFL.

verdict_history.harness: template_v4.c (single WIDTH-byte literal-equality CMP, plain bytewise read, no derivation)
verdict_history.diagnosis: (vp, naive) primary pair reproduced at WIDTH=2/4 (vp/naive ~4-5x) but the FULL 4-fuzzer signature is BRBR (cmp dominates by 12-380x at WIDTH=2/4). Same shape as i2s_magic_number_gate, NOT the B-R- shape of the 13 real-target branches. v4's harness is a magic-number-gate-shaped synthetic where I2S substitution is trivial; doesn't demonstrate the catalog-distinct mechanism of 'VP wins where I2S can't'. Kept as methodology lesson: single-CMP read-then-compare is insufficient to differentiate from i2s_magic_number_gate.

Tried to defeat I2S via XOR-checksum mechanism. With all-zero seed, I2S substitution finds `00 00 00 00` everywhere in input, splices TARGET (0xDEADBEEF) at bytes 0..3 -> chunk0 = TARGET, other chunks still 0 -> checksum = TARGET XOR 0 = TARGET -> trap. Trivial single-splice. cmp produced 7000-10000 crashes per trial via I2S-stage refinement of initial seed alone (queue stuck at 1-3 with crashes 7000+). Methodology lesson: XOR-of-chunks decomposes such that I2S splice is structurally satisfying — cannot defeat I2S this way.

Tried sum-of-bytes near max (TARGET = SUM_WIDTH * 250) to mathematically prevent I2S splice. Two independent failure modes: (a) at low W, trap density too HIGH — naive's random walk hits frequently and dominates because vp/cmp pay execution overhead. (b) at high W, gradient too JITTERY — Hamming distance to TARGET is non-monotone with arithmetic distance (popcount-vs-magnitude mismatch); VP's CMP_MAP best-distance buckets retain seeds at low Hamming distance but those seeds aren't useful stepping stones for further mutation. VP queue grows (gradient signal exists) but doesn't converge to target. naive wins at W=4/8/16; all collapse at W=32. Methodology lesson: single-CMP arithmetic-defeat-of-I2S is HARDER than expected; gradient-vs-arithmetic-distance mismatch breaks VP's walking even on the simplest possible additive checksum. Single-CMP synthetics may not be capable of demonstrating the 'VP wins where cmp can't' mechanism in LibAFL — the multi-CMP coordination of v2 IS what reproduces.

results_per_trial._note: v2 chain harness, full 4-fuzzer table. vp+naive from initial v2 verification (2026-05-03); cmp+vpc from extension run (2026-05-10).

summary.headline: v2 chain harness with cmp+vpc extension REPRODUCES the catalog identity. At CHAIN_DEPTH>=4: cmp = naive = 0 (geometric collapse via bootstrap starvation + dictionary roulette + multi-step coordination); vp wins via K independent CMP_MAP gradient buckets; vpc dominates with monotone-growing synergy (1.1x at K=4 -> 130x at K=16) via VP-bootstraps-corpus + I2S-refines-each-step. Same qualitative shape as all 13 real-target branches.

monotone_curves.cmplog: drops to 0 at K>=4 (1 -> 0 -> 0 -> 0); cmplog stage never activates due to bootstrap starvation
monotone_curves.value_profile: decreasing as gradient walks more cmps (3294 -> 9000 -> 834 -> 39); peak productivity at K=4 then absolute count decays
monotone_curves.value_profile_cmplog: plateau at K=4-8 (10K, 8.5K) then decay to 5K at K=16; vpc/vp ratio GROWS with K (1.1x -> 10x -> 130x) — the synergy mechanism fully reveals at deep chains

summary.deviation_from_v4: v4 single-CMP read-then-compare reproduced (vp, naive) primary pair but full shape was BRBR (cmp dominated 12-380x). v2 chain at K>=4 produces cmp = 0 — the B-R- shape that matches real-target evidence. v4 retired as catalog-distinct demonstrator.
summary.deviation_from_v5_v6: v5 (XOR checksum) refuted by trivial I2S splice. v6 (sum-of-bytes near max) refuted by density-too-high at low W and gradient-too-jittery at high W. Single-CMP arithmetic-defeat-of-I2S is structurally harder than expected in LibAFL; chain-of-narrow-CMPs (v2) is the canonical mechanism.

notes: v2 IS the canonical demonstrator. v4/v5/v6 tested adjacent mechanisms (single-CMP read-then-compare, XOR-checksum, sum-checksum) — none reproduce the real-target 4-fuzzer shape; kept in verdict_history as methodology lessons.

Real-target origin: mbedtls/library/ssl_tls12_client.c branch 378, line 1266 — `if (ssl->tls_version > ssl->conf->max_tls_version || ssl->tls_version < ssl->conf->min_tls_version)`. tls_version is parsed via `mbedtls_ssl_read_version(buf)` (plain BE read for TLS path). The mechanism is NOT bijective derivation (refuted by v3) and NOT pure single-CMP gradient (BRBR shape would emerge — refuted by v4); it IS the multi-stage parser cascade (DTLS record header -> handshake -> version range check) that requires K>=4 sequential literal-equality CMPs at the libafl-feedback layer. n=10: vp 8/10 resolved (avg blocked 1.00h, hits 41.5); naive 0/10 (8/10 unreached). Cmp 1/10 — geometric collapse from K-step coordination as predicted by v2.

The 13 real-target branches all instantiate the v2 mechanism via diverse source patterns: parser cascades (mbedtls TLS, sqlite vacuum keyword cascade), N-way switch dispatch (lcms validDeviceClass 7-arm, sqlite VDBE 100+ opcode arms, libpcap bison reduction switch), tag-table presence cascade (lcms cmsIsMatrixShaper 6-tag check). All reduce at the libafl-feedback layer to K independent literal-equality CMPs whose CMP_MAP buckets VP retains best-distance seeds along.

vpc/vp synergy ratio grows monotonically with CHAIN_DEPTH (1.1x at K=4 -> 10x at K=8 -> 130x at K=16). This is the BOOTSTRAP-RESCUE mechanism: VP grows the corpus via per-CMP gradient buckets; once corpus has multiple entries the cmplog stage activates and I2S splices the K literals into each new corpus entry. Functionally identical to i2s_corpus_pollution's vpc-rescues-cmp pattern but driven by chain depth instead of dictionary pollution.

Methodology lesson from v3: bijective bytewise transforms (XOR with constant) theoretically preserve Hamming distance through the cmp instrumentation, but in LibAFL's actual implementation the gradient does NOT navigate through call boundaries. Even one XOR layer between input read and the cmp drops vp/naive to ~1x. Implication for the catalog: do NOT claim VP rescues programs that perform pre-comparison transforms — claim only the multi-CMP coordination regime.

Methodology lesson from v4-v6: single-CMP synthetics cannot demonstrate 'VP wins where cmp can't' in LibAFL. Either I2S substitution trivially solves (v4 plain equality, v5 XOR-checksum-with-zero-seed) or random walk wins by density (v6 sum near max with low W) or gradient is too narrow/jittery (v6 high W, v3 d=0 single 64-bit). The mechanism that IS demonstrable is multi-CMP coordination (v2 chain), where each individual CMP is byte-narrow (clean gradient) and K of them compounds to defeat I2S's single-substitution-per-cycle limit.
v2 corroboration #1 (origin): mbedtls br 378 — see real-target origin note above.

v2 corroboration #2 (2026-05-03): mbedtls/library/ssl_tls.c branch 372, `ssl->state == MBEDTLS_SSL_SERVER_HELLO` — DTLS handshake state-machine gate. vpc 6/10 vs cmp 1/10. State-machine compounding IS chain compounding from VP's gradient perspective.
v2 corroboration #3 (2026-05-03): mbedtls br 371 ssl_msg.c `tls_version == 0x0302` — DTLS handshake version equality. vpc 7/10, cmp 0/10. Cascade-based mechanism, not mixed-bijection variant.
v2 corroboration #4 (2026-05-03): lcms br 65 `case cmsSigColorSpaceClass:` — 7-arm switch on 4-byte ICC DeviceClass FOURCC. vpc 9/10, cmp 1/10 (dictionary-spread roulette).

v2 corroboration #5 (2026-05-03): sqlite3 br 3236 `if(iA==0)` — VDBE OP_Divide operand check. vp 10/10, naive 0/10. Cascade: SQL parser -> VDBE program build -> opcode dispatch -> operand-type test -> per-case discriminator -> 64-bit zero check. ~5 cascade depth.
v2 corroboration #6 (2026-05-03): sqlite3 br 1898 — Lemon yy_reduce switch on yyruleno. vp 9/10, naive 0/10. Side-B all start with `vacuum `. SQL TOKENIZER KEYWORD CASCADE.
v2 corroboration #7 (2026-05-04): sqlite3 br 1754 sqlite3TwoPartName `if(pName2->n>0)`. Same vacuum cascade, deeper. vp 9/10, naive 0/10. PARSER NAME-RESOLVER POST-REDUCTION.
v2 corroboration #8 (2026-05-04): sqlite3 br 1663 `if(op==TK_CAST || op==TK_UPLUS)` — AST-walker op-field dispatch. vp 7/10, naive 1/10. Side-B all begin `SELECT ` followed by `+`-laden arithmetic.
v2 corroboration #9 (2026-05-04): lcms br 241 `if(cmsIsMatrixShaper(hProfile))` — 6-tag presence cascade. vp 10/10, naive 0/10. TAG-TABLE PRESENCE-TEST CASCADE.
v2 corroboration #10 (2026-05-04): sqlite3 br 1808 `sqlite3Vacuum` body `if(iDb<0)`. Deepest in vacuum cascade. vp 9/10, naive 0/10. VACUUM-CASCADE TERMINAL BUILDER.
v2 corroboration #11 (2026-05-04 night): sqlite3 br 1572 `case OP_ShiftLeft:` VDBE arm (sister of br3236 OP_Divide). vp 4/10, naive 0/10. SECOND VDBE OPCODE-DISPATCH SISTER.

v2 corroboration #12 (2026-05-04 night): lcms br 65 `case cmsSigAbstractClass:` — RECLASSIFIED from i2s_magic_number_gate via per-fuzzer audit. vp 10/10, cmplog 1/10, naive 0/10. 7-ARM SWITCH-CASE DISPATCH.

v2 corroboration #13 (2026-05-04, libpcap br 660): bison-generated yyparse() reduction switch on rule number. vpc 1/3 vs cmp 0/3, ΔAUC=69.5M. PARSER-GENERATOR REDUCTION SWITCH (bison vs Lemon, BPF-filter vs SQL — same mechanism).

v2 corroboration #14 (2026-05-06): lcms br 63 `case cmsSigLinkClass:` — sister arm of br65. vp 10/10, cmplog 8/10, naive 0/10. Within-switch sister-arm cascade-depth invariance for value_profile (vp 10/10 across sister arms); within-switch dictionary-roulette variance for cmplog (cmp 1/10 br65, 8/10 br63 — same dictionary, different draws).

v2 corroboration #15 (2026-05-06): lcms br 62 `case cmsSigOutputClass:` — sister arm of br65 and br63. vp 10/10, naive 0/10. Cross-arm comparison cleanly demonstrates VP's per-arm gradient is dispatch-position-invariant; cmplog's I2SRandReplace is stochastic in dictionary slot.

v2 corroboration #16 (2026-05-06): lcms br 349 `if(cls == cmsSigNamedColorClass)` inside GetXFormColorSpaces. vp 9/10, naive 0/10. Side-B 'nmcl' at 0x0c-0x0f. Same mechanism family as validDeviceClass arms (br62/63/65).
